extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primoz won shortened stage sprint after course cut from 177km to 107km, surviving team positioning chaos before final climb.
tactical_patterns: Course shortened by 70km created positioning chaos at climb base with Jumbo-Visma winning the pre-climb fight for position.
ANACONA Winner attacked from bunch during final climb but Martinez breakaway caught, nullifying early break importance completely.
GEOGHEGAN HART Hugh moved with 300m but mistimed sprint badly against Roglic's late power on downhill finish.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Won second sprint finish in four stages, demonstrating unexpected sprint power rather than GC-only focus
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Third place shows Tour de France preparation progressing after poor year start, but couldn't match Roglic sprint
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Hugh: negative: Initiated sprint 300m out but lacked finishing power to convert positioning advantage into first pro win
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: neutral: Unable to come around despite good positioning behind Geoghegan Hart's early sprint initiation
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Daniel: negative: Most important breakaway rider caught after Anacona's bunch attack, breakaway completely neutralized on climb
